project.title: Regulatory role of vitamin D3 on myogenesis and fibrogenesis under Vdr gene silencing and TGF-β1 stimulation in skeletal muscle cells
project.projectDescription: Vitamin D deficiency is associated with a decline in muscle function and an increasing risk of muscle injury in athletes and the elderly. Nevertheless, how vitamin D3 and vitamin D receptor (VDR) regulate skeletal muscle cells under pro-fibrotic factor stimulation that could be pronounced during repetitive muscle damage have not been elucidated. Therefore, this study aimed to investigate the regulatory role of cholecalciferol (D3), calcidiol (25D3), calcitriol (1,25D3), and the effect of Vdr gene suppression under TGF-β1 stimulation in C2C12 mouse skeletal muscle cells. All forms of vitamin D3 exerted anti-fibrotic effects under TGF-β1 stimulation by suppression of COL1A1; however, D3 preserves this effect without a negative impact on myogenesis. Moreover, LC-MS/MS-based proteomics analysis revealed that myoblast fusion protein and mitochondrial regulation were altered following Vdr knockdown. These changes were associated with exacerbation of α-SMA expression in TGF-β1-treated cells and suggested VDR modulates fibrogenesis in skeletal muscle cells regardless of ligand binding. Under TGF-β1 stimulation, anti-fibrotic effects of 1,25D3 but not D3 were diminished after Vdr knockdown, supporting D3 action is not dependent on VDR activation. Collectively, understanding anti-fibrotic effects of vitamin D3 is beneficial for providing a strategy of vitamin D supplementation to counteract fibrosis development after muscle injury.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Briefly, proteins were precipitated with acetone at -20C overnight. Protein pellets were collected by centrifugation and air-dried before resolubilizing with 0.1% RapiGest SF (186001861) (Waters, MA, USA). The protein solution was mixed with a 10 mM Tris (2-carboxyethyl) phosphine hydrochloride solution (C4706) (Sigma-Aldrich) and subsequently mixed with 50 mM iodoacetamide (I1149) (Sigma-Aldrich) before being cleaned up using a desalting column (89882) (Thermo Scientific). The flow-through fraction was mixed with the Trypsin/Lys-c solution (100 ng/ul) (V5073) (Promega, WI, USA) at 37C overnight. The digested peptides were dried by speed vacuum and reconstituted in 0.1% formic acid (A117-50) (Fisher Chemical, NJ, USA). 1.5 µg of peptides was subjected to LC-MS/MS and processed using an Orbitrap Q Exactive HF hybrid mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific) combined with an Dionex UltiMate 3000 RSLCnano System (Thermo Scientific) at the Central Instrument Facility, Faculty of Science, Mahidol University.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The acquired MS/MS spectra (RAW file) were searched against the Mus musculus protein database and processed with Proteome Discoverer software (Thermo Scientific).
